zh.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton是Ranulph I De Sylvester和Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester的兒子，於1120年出生在英國爾斯托雷頓。他於1145年之前與Mrs Alan de Sylvester在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1170年在他的家鄉享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton是Ranulph I De Sylvester和Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester的儿子，于1120年出生在英国爾斯托雷頓。他于1145年之前与Mrs Alan de Sylvester在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1170年在他的家乡享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sylvester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: from the Middle English and Latin personal name Silvester ‘dweller in the forest’ (a derivative of silva ‘wood’). The name was borne by three popes, including a contemporary of Constantine the Great, and seems to have been first used in England by clerics.

variants.name: Silvestre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Fernando, Juan, Miguel, Carlos, Ramon, Raul, Efren, Josefina, Mario, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim.
Galician and Spanish: habitational name from any of the places called Silvestre in Pontevedra and Badajoz.
Catalan, French (Occitan), and Spanish: from the personal name Silvestre (see Sylvester ).

variants.name: Selwyn
meanings: English (Gloucestershire):
from the Middle English personal name Selewin(e) (Old English Selewine, perhaps from sele ‘hall’ or sǣl ‘happiness, prosperity’ + wine ‘friend’).

of Norman origin, perhaps from the Middle English and Old French personal name Selvein, Silvein (from Latin Silvanus, an adjective from silva ‘woodland’), with vocalization of /v/ to /w/, possibly through influence of the name above. In Anglo-Norman usage both Selvein and Silvester were sometimes Latinized as Salvagius ‘wild, savage’ (see Sylvester and Savage ), though whether the vernacular surnames were ever interchangeable is a question for further research.

variants.name: West
meanings: English and German: from Middle English, Middle High German west ‘west’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived to the west of a settlement, or a habitational name for someone who had migrated from further west.
Americanized form of Finnish Vesterinen: from the personal name Vesteri (a short form of Sylvester ) + the surname suffix -nen.

History: This name was brought to North America independently by many bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Thomas West, 12th Baron De La Warre, was captain general of Virginia in 1610–11. The state of DE is named for him. One of the earliest permanent settlers was Francis West (1606–92), who came to Duxbury, MA, from Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, in or before 1638.

variants.name: Sylvestre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Pierre, Adelard, Aime, Alphonse, Carmelle, Donat, Gilles, Jean Claude, Marcel.
French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Sylvestre (see Sylvester ).
History: Nicolas Sylvestre dit Champagne from Pont-sur-Seine in Aube, France, married Barbe Neveu in Quebec City, QC, in 1667.

variants.name: Sule
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Aparna, Dileep, Geeta, Harsh, Pushkar, Sangeeta, Vikram.  Hungarian Mihaly, Sandor.
Indian (Maharashtra; pronounced as two syllables): Maratha name, from Marathi suḷa ‘pointed tooth’, from Sanskrit šūla ‘spike, spear’.
Hungarian: from a pet form of the personal name Szilveszter (see Sylvester ).

variants.name: Silvester
meanings: English and German: variant of   Sylvester  .

variants.name: Silvestro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Angelo, Sal, Caesar, Agostino, Antonio, Camillo, Carmelo, Dino, Ennio, Gaetano, Giovanni.
Italian: from the personal name Silvestro, Latin Silvester (see Sylvester ).

variants.name: Sylvest
meanings: Danish: from a personal name, a shortened form of Latin Silvester (see Sylvester ).

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

en.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton was born in 1120, in Storeton, Cheshire, England as the son of Ranulph I De Sylvester and Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester. He married Mrs Alan de Sylvester before 1145, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1170, in his hometown, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton est né en 1120, à Storeton, Cheshire, Angleterre, fils de Ranulph I De Sylvester et Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester. Il a épousé Mrs Alan de Sylvester avant 1145, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1170, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton wurde 1120 in Storeton, Cheshire, England, als Sohn von Ranulph I De Sylvester und Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester geboren. Er hat vor 1145 in England Mrs Alan de Sylvester geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1170 im Alter von 50 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton è nato il nel 1120 nel Storeton, Cheshire, England come figlio di Ranulph I De Sylvester e Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester. Lui sposato Mrs Alan de Sylvester prima del 1145, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1170, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.Storeton, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアストーレトン
ja.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourtonは1120年にイギリスチェシュアイアストーレトンでRanulph I De SylvesterとMrs Ranulph I De Sylvesterの息子として生まれた。1145年以前にイギリスでMrs Alan de Sylvesterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1170年に故郷で50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton(은)는 1120년 영국 체실르 스토레턴에서 Ranulph I De Sylvester(와)과 Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1145년 이전 영국에서 Mrs Alan de Sylvester(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1170년 그의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton nasceu em 1120, em Storeton, Cheshire, Inglaterra filho de Ranulph I De Sylvester e Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester. Ele casou-se com Mrs Alan de Sylvester antes 1145, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1170, em sua cidade natal, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton родился в 1120 г. в местности сторетон, чешир, Англия. Его родителей звали Ranulph I De Sylvester и Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Alan de Sylvester перед 1145 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1170 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Alan de Sylvester Master of the Forest of the Wirral, Lord of Stourton nació en 1120, en Storeton, Cheshire, Inglaterra como hijo de Ranulph I De Sylvester y Mrs Ranulph I De Sylvester. Se casó con Mrs Alan de Sylvester antes de 1145, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1170, en su pueblo, a la edad de 50 años.
